facts_disclosed: Has acid reflux — takes omeprazole 20mg every morning before going to market

turns.content: random question na ka 😅 do you know what food is okay to eat late at night if you have stomach problem?? I just ate pad krapao at like 11pm and now my chest is burning

Ouch — that burning sounds like acid reflux. Pad krapao late at night is a tough combo: spicy, oily, and right before lying down. What kind of stomach problems do you usually have? That'll help me give better advice.

yes acid reflux na ka!! haha so ironic right. I cook food all day long — I am a street food vendor — but my own stomach cannot handle spicy food at night. the doctor diagnosed me maybe 2 years ago. she give me omeprazole. 20mg. I take it every morning before I go to the market

Ha, that IS ironic — spending all day around food and then having your stomach revolt against it! At least you're on omeprazole, which is good for managing the acid. 20mg is a standard dose.

But yeah, eating spicy food at 11pm is going to override what the omeprazole does, especially if you lie down soon after. For late-night eating with acid reflux, here are some safer options:

- **Banana** — naturally antacid, easy on the stomach
- **Plain congee or jok** — gentle, warm, soothing
- **Crackers or plain bread** — absorbs acid
- **Yogurt** — can help coat the stomach

Avoid spicy, fried, citrus, and tomato-heavy stuff at night. How long after eating do you usually go to bed?

usually I eat and then go to bed like 30 minutes later because I wake up at 4am na ka so I need to sleep early!! but sometimes I am so hungry after cleaning the cart that I just eat whatever is fast. pad krapao from the lady next to my spot, som tum from the cart across the road... all spicy haha

That's the problem right there — 30 minutes between eating and lying down is way too short for someone with acid reflux. The acid doesn't have time to settle and just comes right back up when you're horizontal.

With your 4am wake-up, I get that you can't stay up late. A couple of suggestions:

1. **Eat your last meal earlier** — even 8 or 9pm instead of 11pm. Can you eat something while you're still at your cart before closing up?
2. **If you must eat late, pick bland foods** — keep bananas, bread, or crackers at home for exactly these moments
3. **Elevate your head when sleeping** — an extra pillow helps keep the acid down
4. **Don't lie flat** — try propping the head of your bed up a few inches

Does the omeprazole usually keep things under control during the day?

during the day is okay na ka. I take the pill at 4am before market and it work fine. I can smell all the food cooking and eat lunch, no problem. but by nighttime the medicine wear off I think. and I am Thai na ka!! everything is spicy. what can I do haha. I cannot eat bland food forever

Haha I hear you — telling a Thai person to stop eating spicy food is basically asking them to change their identity! You don't have to go bland forever, but it's about timing. Spicy for lunch when your omeprazole is at peak effectiveness? Totally fine. Spicy at 11pm when the medication has worn off and you're about to lie down? That's when you get tonight's situation.

One thing you could ask your doctor about: some people take a second lower dose of omeprazole in the evening for better overnight coverage. But that's a conversation for your doctor, not me.

For now, how about this compromise: keep some jok packets at home. When you get home hungry at 10-11pm, make a quick bowl of jok instead of ordering pad krapao. Still warm, still satisfying, but won't set your chest on fire.

jok is actually a good idea!! I can make jok in like 10 minutes na ka. I literally cook rice all day so making jok at home is easy haha. okay so question — the burning right now, what should I do?? I already take omeprazole this morning so I cannot take another one right??

Right, don't take another omeprazole — it's a once-daily medication and doubling up isn't recommended without your doctor saying so.

For right now, try these to get some relief:

1. **Drink a glass of warm water** — not cold, not hot, just warm. It helps dilute the acid
2. **Don't lie down yet** — sit upright or semi-reclined for at least another 30-45 minutes if you can manage it
3. **Antacid tablets** — do you have any at home? Something like Gavicon or even basic antacid tablets? Those are separate from omeprazole and safe to take for immediate relief
4. **Chew gum if you have it** — increases saliva which neutralizes acid

If you don't have antacids at home, I'd suggest buying a small pack to keep around for exactly these late-night situations.

I think I have Gaviscon somewhere!! let me check... yes!! okay I take this. and I will sit up for a bit. arai wa 4am is only 5 hours away and I am still awake with burning chest 😩 this is what I get for eating pad krapao at 11pm

The Gaviscon should help within about 10-15 minutes — it forms a barrier on top of the acid so it can't come back up. Take it and sit upright while it kicks in.

And yes, lesson learned! The pad krapao lady is probably great but she's not your friend at 11pm. Save her for lunch. 😄

Here's your new nighttime routine:
- Keep Gaviscon stocked at home as backup
- Keep jok packets or bananas for late-night hunger
- Try to eat your last real meal by 8-9pm at the cart
- When you lie down, prop up with an extra pillow

Now take the Gaviscon, sit up for 15-20 minutes, and then try to get some sleep. You've got a market run in 5 hours!

okay sitting up now with Gaviscon. waiting for it to work. you know what bother me is the doctor say acid reflux can get worse if I don't manage it. like the throat can get damaged. I am a food vendor na ka I NEED my throat to yell at customers haha 'khao man gai aroi na kaaaa!!' 😂

Your doctor is right — chronic acid reflux can damage the esophagus over time if it's not managed. The acid keeps irritating the lining and that can lead to problems. So it's actually important to take it seriously, even though it feels minor.

The good news is you're already on omeprazole which does the heavy lifting. You just need to help it out by not sabotaging it at 11pm with pad krapao! Your throat needs to stay in prime khao man gai yelling condition.

Honestly though, the timing thing is the biggest change you can make. Take the omeprazole in the morning like you do, eat whatever you want for lunch, but by evening just ease off the spicy stuff. That one change alone would probably cut your flare-ups in half.

haha okay okay I take this seriously na ka. you know what is funny, I tell my customers 'eat eat eat!!' all day but my own body say 'please stop eating.' the irony of my life 😂 okay the Gaviscon is working, the burning going down. thank you so much!! I will buy jok packets tomorrow. and I promise to stop eating spicy at 11pm. maybe. 555 goodnight!!

Haha "maybe" — I knew that promise had a time limit! But at least try the jok thing. Your stomach will thank you.

Glad the Gaviscon is kicking in. And the irony is honestly hilarious — a food vendor whose own stomach fights back. You should make it a reel: "POV: you cook 200 plates a day but your stomach says no to dinner" 😂

Goodnight! Get whatever sleep you can before that 4am alarm. Take care! 🙏
